seoTitle: Green Pigeon vs Green Point — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Green Pigeon and Green Point. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Green Pigeon vs Green Point: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Green Pigeon and Green Point.
heading: Green Pigeon vs Green Point — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Green Pigeon, New South Wales and Green Point, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Green Pigeon has a lower overall climate risk score (39/100) compared to Green Point (43/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Green Pigeon or Green Point safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Green Pigeon has a flood risk score of 49/100, while Green Point scores 38/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Green Pigeon scores 29/100 for bushfire risk. Green Point scores 28/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Green Pigeon or Green Point?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Green Pigeon has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.88% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
